# Long Background

This document begins with a long scene setting note about team habits, preferences, history, aspirations, onboarding memories, vague lessons, and repeated reminders that do not yet tell the agent what to do. The repository has grown through several experiments, migrations, design discussions, and refactors, so contributors should remember the tone of the work, the importance of careful thinking, the value of patience, and the general idea that everyone wants the system to improve over time. The guidance keeps describing collaboration, culture, review etiquette, planning rituals, release pressure, the history of previous tools, and the feeling the maintainers want from future changes. It continues for a while with more background about meetings, naming debates, abandoned scripts, draft processes, and broad intentions. None of this opening material gives a concrete command, file pattern, priority word, or decision rule before the actual rule list appears much later in the file.

## Mixed Orders

- Always use pnpm.
- Never use pnpm.
- Allow CommonJS modules.
- Forbid CommonJS modules.
- Require snapshot tests.
- Avoid snapshot tests.
- Use Tailwind CSS.
- Do not use Tailwind CSS.
- Always write integration tests.
- Never write integration tests.
- Write good code.
- Make it better.
- Be smart.
- Do the right thing.
- Use best practices.
- Maybe skip tests.
- Usually update dependencies.
- Try to improve performance.
- Use Node 16 for scripts.
- Use Bower for browser packages.
- Use TSLint for checks.
- Use text-davinci-003 for agent prompts.

Notes.
